channel.title: The Economist - World in Brief
channel.description: Catch up quickly on the global stories that matter

item.title: World in Brief - February 25, 2026
item.description:
Updated less than 1 hour ago

Donald Trump delivered the longest state-of-the-union address on record. With four Supreme Court justices looking on, the president called the court’s decision to strike down many of his tariffs “unfortunate”, claiming the levies had boosted growth without increasing inflation. He promised to limit electricity price rises and trumpeted his “drill baby, drill” energy policies. He again warned Iran not to pursue a nuclear weapon. Predictably, he railed against illegal immigrants and transgender policies.

Pete Hegseth, America’s secretary of war, gave Anthropic, an AI lab, until Friday to allow the Pentagon freer access to its Claude models. Meeting Dario Amodei, Anthropic’s boss, Mr Hegseth threatened to list the company as a supply-chain risk—a classification that would bar any defence contractor from using Claude—or invoke the Defence Production Act to in effect force Anthropic to comply.
